title: truth-table.json — the AA-0 capability truth table
description: Stage AA-0's deliverable (docs/ARM-ALTRA.md §AA-0): what the delivered Ampere Altra actually exposes, recorded from real silicon as expect-vs-found rows. Its acceptance is that the table is complete and machine-readable, BYTE-IDENTICAL ACROSS TWO REBOOTS, and that every `expect` row is either confirmed or recorded as a deviation with an explicit disposition — including a FAVOURABLE deviation (ECV unexpectedly present would still require a recorded ruling before any stage relied on it). Because byte-identity across reboots is the acceptance, this schema carries no capture-specific field: the capture identity lives in the path (`results/aa-0/<capture>/truth-table.json`), never in the bytes, so that two captures can be diffed directly. UNTESTED ON SILICON: no truth table matching this schema has ever been produced by hardware; the `expect` column is what docs/ARM-ALTRA.md predicts, and the whole point of AA-0 is that predictions are not findings.

schema_version.description: Bumped when a field's meaning changes. A reader refuses a version it does not know rather than silently misreading it.

rows.description: The expect-vs-found rows. Every mandatory row below must be present; extra rows are welcome (an unlisted capability that turned out to matter is a finding, not a schema error). The thirteen mandatory ids are exactly the facts docs/ARM-ALTRA.md §AA-0 requires — INCLUDING the two existential work-clock rows (`perf_event_open` of raw 0x21 as a pinned, non-multiplexed event SUCCEEDS; a trivial host-side overflow test DELIVERS a sample/signal) and the writable-ID-register surface. Those three were once absent from this list, which meant a schema-valid truth table could leave the PMU-open, overflow-delivery and writable-ID facts unmeasured — and AA-1, the whole point of AA-0, rests on the first two.

identity.description: Who this machine is. Recorded from the silicon, never from the procurement order.

part_num.description: `MIDR_EL1.PartNum` (0xd0c = Neoverse N1). Recorded, not asserted: a box that is not the N1 we think it is must be visible as such.

soc.description: The SoC part as the platform reports it (e.g. an Ampere Altra Q80-30).

core_count.description: Online cores. N1 cores are single-threaded — there is no SMT sibling to idle, and one whole x86 confound class is absent.

firmware.description: Firmware versions, sorted key/value (never a hash map: these bytes are diffed across reboots).

topology.description: The standing core-assignment table for this box (docs/BOX-PINNING.md discipline, Altra edition). Pinning is a correctness condition on this lineage (rr #3607), so the assignment is evidence, not hygiene.

governor.description: The cpufreq governor the box stands at. V-time counts are frequency-independent; this is recorded so that claim is checkable.

row.description: One expect-vs-found row. `confirmed` must be true exactly when `found` equals `expected`; when it is false the row is a DEVIATION and `disposition` must carry the explicit ruling AA-0's acceptance demands.

id.description: Stable row identifier. The mandatory ones are named in the `rows` constraint above; these are the ones docs/ARM-ALTRA.md §AA-0 requires.

kind.description: Which surface the row was read from.

question.description: The row in one sentence, with its architectural source (e.g. `ID_AA64MMFR0_EL1.ECV — is FEAT_ECV present? Expect absent: Armv8.2 predates it, and no reachable ARM server part has it.`).

expected.description: What docs/ARM-ALTRA.md predicts. `absent` for ECV / SVE / nested-virt; `present` for LSE, BR_RETIRED-in-PMCEID0, /dev/kvm, KVM_CAP_SET_GUEST_DEBUG and a creatable vGICv3; a free string for graded facts (PMUVer, VHE-vs-nVHE).

found.description: What the silicon said. The whole reason this stage exists.

raw.description: The raw evidence behind `found` — the register field value, the `PMCEID0_EL0` bit, the ioctl return. Free-form, but never empty: a row with no raw evidence is an assertion, not a reading.

confirmed.description: `found == expected`. False makes this row a deviation.

disposition.description: The explicit recorded ruling for a deviation — required whenever `confirmed` is false, INCLUDING a favourable deviation. `null` only on a confirmed row.
